title: Melted Magic: National Grilled Cheese Day
story: Sizzling butter hits the pan as millions of people celebrate National Grilled Cheese Sandwich Day on April 12! This gooey holiday brings families together in kitchens everywhere, flipping golden-brown sandwiches filled with melted cheese. title: Love It or Hate It Licorice Day
story: Black licorice might be the most divisive candy on Earth, and April 12 is when it gets its moment to shine! National Licorice Day celebrates this polarizing treat that people either absolutely love or completely despise. activity_answer: People either love it or hate it completely

title: Blast Off to Russian Cosmonautics Day!
story: Every April 12, Russia celebrates Cosmonautics Day to honor their amazing space achievements! This special day marks when Yuri Gagarin became the first human to travel into space back in 1961. Russians celebrate by visiting space museums, watching rocket launches on TV, and learning about astronauts called cosmonauts. Schools organize space-themed activities where kids build model rockets and study the solar system. Many people share photos of stars and planets on social media. People around the globe mark this day by visiting space museums, watching rocket launches online, and sharing photos of the stars. title: Space Mission's Unexpected Twist
story: Engines roared as Soyuz 33 plummeted through Earth's atmosphere in 1979. The spacecraft's journey took an unexpected turn on April 12 when a malfunction forced an early return. Despite the setback, the crew's quick thinking and backup systems ensured a safe landing. This mission highlighted the importance of rigorous training and safety measures in space exploration.
activity_question: What happened to Soyuz 33 on April 12, 1979?
activity_choices: It docked with a space station
follow_up_question: Why is it important for astronauts to train for unexpected situations in space?

title: A Milestone in the Global Health Battle
story: The world faced a huge challenge in 2022. On April 12, known COVID-19 cases reached 500 million worldwide. But there was hope too. By then, about 5 billion people had gotten at least one vaccine shot. That's nearly two-thirds of everyone on Earth! Scientists, doctors, and regular folks all worked together to fight the virus.
activity_question: How many people had received at least one vaccine shot by April 12, 2022?
follow_up_question: Why do you think vaccines were important in fighting COVID-19?

title: Frosty Disaster Strikes French Vineyards
story: Icy fingers crept across France in 2021, bringing the worst frost in decades. On April 12, wine experts announced a chilling discovery: the freezing temperatures would damage most of the country's precious grapevines. Farmers scrambled to protect their crops, lighting fires and using heaters to ward off the cold. Despite their efforts, the frost affected about four-fifths of French vineyards.
activity_question: What did farmers do to try to protect their grapevines?
activity_choices: Played loud music to scare frost away
follow_up_question: Why do you think frost is so dangerous for grapevines?

title: Underdogs Triumph in Stanley Cup Shocker
story: Hockey fans were stunned in 1938 when the Chicago Black Hawks pulled off an incredible upset. Despite a losing record in the regular season, they skated to victory on April 12. The Black Hawks defeated the Toronto Maple Leafs 4-1, clinching the Stanley Cup with a 3-1 series win. It was a true underdog story, as no other team has ever won the Cup with a losing season record.
activity_question: What team did the Chicago Black Hawks defeat to win the Stanley Cup?
follow_up_question: Why was the Black Hawks' victory considered unusual?

title: Bruins Sweep Red Wings for Stanley Cup
story: Hockey fans roared as the Boston Bruins clinched the Stanley Cup in 1941. On April 12, they defeated the Detroit Red Wings 3-1 at Olympia Stadium in Detroit. This victory completed a perfect 4-0 series sweep for the Bruins. The team's dominant performance thrilled supporters and showcased their skill on the ice.
activity_question: Where did the final game of the 1941 Stanley Cup take place?
bonus_fact: The Stanley Cup is the oldest professional sports trophy in North America.

title: Hoops History: A Low-Scoring Showdown
story: Squeaking sneakers and clanging rims echoed through the arena in 1992. The Detroit Pistons and New York Knicks faced off in a defensive battle for the ages on April 12. When the final buzzer sounded, the scoreboard showed a jaw-dropping 72-61 victory for Detroit. This nail-biter became the second-lowest scoring game in NBA history!
activity_question: Which team won this low-scoring game?
follow_up_question: Why do you think the score was so low in this game?

title: Shaky Ground in Germany
story: The earth rumbled and buildings swayed as a powerful earthquake struck Germany in 1992. On April 12, people felt the ground move beneath their feet. It was one of the strongest quakes to hit the country in years. Despite the scary shaking, Germans showed their strength by helping each other and quickly starting to rebuild.
activity_question: What happened in Germany on April 12, 1992?
follow_up_question: How did the German people respond to this natural disaster?

title: Broadway's Big Night: Tony Awards Triumph
story: The stage lights shone bright on April 12, 1959, as Broadway celebrated its best. The 13th Tony Awards dazzled with talent and creativity. Two shows stole the spotlight that night. 'J.B.', a powerful play, claimed top honors. Meanwhile, the lively musical 'Redhead' twirled its way to victory. These wins marked a memorable moment in theater history.
activity_question: Which two shows won big at the 13th Tony Awards?
follow_up_question: What do you think makes a play or musical award-worthy?

title: Keiko Fukuda: A Judo Pioneer
story: Keiko Fukuda kicked her way into history on April 12, 1913. Born in Tokyo, Japan, she became a martial arts legend. Fukuda dedicated her life to judo, breaking barriers for women in the sport. She moved to America and continued teaching, inspiring students with her skill and wisdom. Her legacy as a trailblazing judoka lives on, encouraging others to pursue their passions fearlessly.
activity_question: Where was Keiko Fukuda born?
bonus_fact: Judo means 'gentle way' in Japanese, focusing on using an opponent's energy against them.

title: Opera Star's Surprising Pop Duet
story: Montserrat Caballé's powerful voice soared beyond opera halls. Born in Barcelona, Spain on April 12, 1933, she became a renowned soprano. But Caballé surprised fans when she teamed up with rock legend Freddie Mercury. Their duet 'Barcelona' blended opera and pop, showcasing her versatility. This unexpected collaboration introduced her incredible talent to a whole new audience.
activity_question: What song did Montserrat Caballé perform with Freddie Mercury?
follow_up_question: What type of singer was Montserrat Caballé primarily known as?

title: A Soviet Space Hero Takes Flight
story: Igor Volk soared into the world on April 12, 1937, in Zmiiv, Ukraine. This future Soviet cosmonaut would grow up to pilot the Soyuz T-12 spacecraft. Volk's journey from Earth to the stars showed how determination and bravery could launch dreams skyward. His work as a test pilot helped push the boundaries of flight, inspiring a new generation of space explorers.
activity_question: What spacecraft did Igor Volk pilot?
follow_up_question: What other jobs do you think help prepare someone to become a cosmonaut?
